primarySourceLabel: UW Tacoma Arts, Media and Culture overview
sourceLabel: UW Tacoma Arts, Media and Culture overview
requirementCueLines: Arts, Media & Culture majors have five tracks to choose from:

For a BA in Arts, Media & Culture, you need to complete 60 credits, which must include a minimum of 30 credits of upper-division (300–400 level) courses, as well as the UWT general education and graduation requirements totaling a minimum of 180 credits.

ownerTitle: Arts, Media and Culture (BA) - American Cultures Track
requirementCueLines: TLIT 220 Literature and the Arts OR
History (One class from List A and one of the following: THIST 220, 221, 320, 340, 349 or TLAX 333) (10 credits)
THIST 487 Technology in the Modern World
TWOMN 345 Women and Work in the United States
Culture (List B; must be 25 credits not taken as part of another requirement for the major) (25 credits)
TAMST 101 American Art, Place and Space
TAMST 210 American Cultures and Perspectives: Class, Ethnicity, Gender and Race
TAMST 250 Science Fiction in American Culture
TRELIG 350 Philosophy, Religion and the Environment
TWRT 340 Asian American Rhetorics, Literacies and Activism
TARTS 200 Studio Foundation I: Contemporary Art Strategies
TARTS 280 3-D Art and Contemporary Approaches to Sculpture
TARTS 320 Improvisational Theater and Performance in Everyday Life
TARTS 367 Ideas Through Objects: A Conceptual Approach to Art
Lists E, F or G (10 credits)
TLAX 355 Migration and Transnational Family in Latino Literature and Film

ownerTitle: Arts, Media and Culture (BA) - Comparative Arts Track
requirementCueLines: Comparative Arts Track | Culture, Arts & Communication | University of Washington Tacoma
TLIT 220 Literature and the Arts OR
History (List A: one survey class and one non-survey history class) (10 credits)
THIST 487 Technology in the Modern World
TWOMN 345 Women and Work in the United States
TAMST 101 American Art, Place and Space
TAMST 210 American Cultures and Perspectives: Class, Ethnicity, Gender and Race
TAMST 250 Science Fiction in American Culture
TRELIG 350 Philosophy, Religion and the Environment
TWRT 340 Asian American Rhetorics, Literacies and Activism
Interpretation (one class from two of each: Literature, Film and Media or Visual and Performing Arts) (10 credits)
This list includes courses that provide language and concepts for the critical study of texts and objects. Courses taken in this list cannot be double-counted to satisfy other requirements.
TLIT 237 Introduction to Literature and Environment
TLIT 251 Ancient Literature of Western Civilization
TLIT 252 Medieval Literature of Western Civilization
TLIT 253 Modern Literature of Western Civilization
Interpretation for Film and Media Track
Interpretation for Visual and Performing Arts Track
TARTS 200 Studio Foundation I: Contemporary Art Strategies
TARTS 280 3-D Art and Contemporary Approaches to Sculpture
TARTS 320 Improvisational Theater and Performance in Everyday Life
TARTS 367 Ideas Through Objects: A Conceptual Approach to Art
Literature (List E), Film and Media (List F) or Visual and Performing Arts (List G) (25 credits)
TLAX 355 Migration and Transnational Family in Latino Literature and Film
